MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF DE LI's Motion To Tax Defendant Yu Kwan Construction Co.'s Costs.

Plaintiff De Xin Li's motion to tax costs is granted. No opposition filed. All costs claimed in the costs memorandum filed by Defendant Yu Kwan Construction Co. on August 8, 2018 are taxed.

Counsel for Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which is verbatim and states that the motion was not opposed.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendants Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar May-21-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Andrew H. Wolff, Tel. No. 1-510-834-3300 appearing for the Plaintiff by telephone appeareance;
Atty. Jeffrey G. Nevin of the Nevin Law Firm, Tel. No. 1-510-350-7338 appearing for
the Defendant.


RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant M&D Lau Corporation's motion for summary judgement is granted. M&D satisfied its summary judgment burden of showing that it did not directly hire and was not the employer of either plaintiff De Xin Lin or the person who dropped the metal plate and did not have any actual or constructive knowledge of any dangerous condition regarding the metal plate. Plaintiff did not submit any evidence creating a triable dispute as to who hired or employed plaintiff or the person who dropped the metal plate or M&D's knowledge. The undisputed facts establish that M&D Lau hired Yu Kwan Construction Co., who in turn employed plaintiff and the person who dropped the metal plate and M&D did not retain control over the work performed by plaintiff and the person who dropped the metal plate. Per Privette and its progeny, M&D owed no duty to plaintiff with respect to his working conditions. Plaintiff's request for a continuance of the hearing to take discovery per CCP 437c(h) is denied because plaintiff fails to show that any evidence may exist to allow him to defeat this motion and, in all events, plaintiff has not shown any diligence in seeking to obtain any such evidence.
Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/hk
